settings.title: The One Step That Makes Spices Taste Like Ours
settings.excerpt: Most home cooks skip it, and it is the difference between flat and fragrant.
settings.body:
Guests often ask why the food at Saffron tastes deeper than what they make at home with the same spices. Usually it comes down to one step.

Bloom your spices in hot fat. Before anything else goes in the pan, let your ground spices sizzle in oil or butter for thirty seconds until they smell fragrant.

This wakes up the oils inside them. Adding them straight to a wet sauce, by contrast, leaves them tasting dusty.

Watch the heat and keep them moving, because spices can scorch in seconds. Once you smell them, the next ingredient goes in fast.
